levels.name: None recognized
levels.explainer: No common-law tort or statutory cause of action for first-party bad faith.

levels.name: Narrow / statute-only, no PRoA
levels.explainer: Limited remedy available only via statute (e.g., IL §155, NY Bi-Economy) without a freestanding tort PRoA.

levels.name: Tort recognized, significant limits
levels.explainer: Common-law tort exists but is narrowed by judicial doctrine, scope limits, or higher burden.

levels.name: Tort well-established, broadly available
levels.explainer: Tort cause of action is firmly recognized for first-party insureds with reasonable scope and damages.

levels.name: Firm + broad damages + lenient standard
levels.explainer: Tort firmly established with broad damages and lenient liability standard (CA Gruenberg/Egan, MT, AZ Noble).

levels.name: No duty-to-settle cause of action
levels.explainer: Liability insurer is not exposed to extra-contractual liability for failure to settle within limits.

levels.name: Limited Stowers/Crisci doctrine
levels.explainer: A narrow doctrinal route exists but with significant constraints on damages or standard.

levels.name: Standard duty-to-settle doctrine
levels.explainer: Mainstream Stowers/Crisci-line cause of action; full excess judgment generally recoverable.

levels.name: Strong duty-to-settle, broad damages
levels.explainer: Robust doctrine with low burden, broad damages, and clear plaintiff path (CA Crisci, TX Stowers).

levels.name: No statutory PRoA
levels.explainer: Statute is regulatory-only; no private right of action under it (CA post-Moradi-Shalal, OR, MN).

levels.name: Limited PRoA, narrow scope
levels.explainer: PRoA exists but tied to specific narrow conduct, or only first-party OR third-party (not both).

levels.name: Broad PRoA, arduous hurdles
levels.explainer: PRoA reaches both first- and third-party but with significant procedural hurdles.

levels.name: Broad PRoA, modest hurdles
levels.explainer: Broadly available across both with modest cure/notice (e.g., FL §624.155 60-day CRN).

levels.name: Broad + low hurdles + mandatory remedies
levels.explainer: Broadly available, low hurdles, mandatory remedies (WA IFCA, TX Ch. 541/542).

levels.name: Actual malice / clear-and-convincing
levels.explainer: Plaintiff must prove subjective bad intent, often by clear-and-convincing evidence.

levels.name: Reckless or willful
levels.explainer: Standard requires reckless disregard or knowing wrongdoing; the practical burden is high.

levels.name: No reasonable basis
levels.explainer: Plaintiff need only show insurer lacked a reasonable basis for the denial or underpayment.

levels.name: Negligence-equivalent or strict
levels.explainer: Negligence-like or strict-liability-like standard for certain statutory unfair claim practices.

levels.name: Contract damages only
levels.explainer: No extra-contractual damages; insured limited to policy proceeds and prejudgment interest.

levels.name: Narrow consequentials only
levels.explainer: Limited consequential damages in narrow circumstances; emotional distress not recoverable.

levels.name: Standard consequentials
levels.explainer: Consequential and emotional-distress damages available; excess-of-policy capped or unavailable.

levels.name: Full consequentials + excess (third-party)
levels.explainer: Broad consequential damages and full excess judgment available in third-party context.

levels.name: Broad damages, no cap
levels.explainer: All categories available with no statutory cap; expansive judicial doctrine.

levels.name: Unavailable
levels.explainer: Punitive damages categorically unavailable in bad-faith actions.

levels.name: Available, strict / hard caps
levels.explainer: Available only on clear-and-convincing 'malice' showing with low statutory cap.

levels.name: Available, moderate caps
levels.explainer: Generally available on standard malice/recklessness showing; meaningful but capped (e.g., 3x compensatory).

levels.name: Broadly available, no cap
levels.explainer: Available on standard showing with no statutory cap or a very high cap.

levels.name: None
levels.explainer: No statutory penalty, multiplier, or premium-interest provision.

levels.name: Modest interest premium
levels.explainer: Statute adds a modest interest premium on overdue payments.

levels.name: Significant interest or modest %
levels.explainer: 12%+ interest or a modest percent penalty layered on the underlying claim.

levels.name: Multiplier ≥1.5x or large %
levels.explainer: Statutory multiplier of 1.5x+ or large percent penalty (GA 50%, TN 25%, NM treble via UPA).

levels.name: Treble or 2–3x, low friction
levels.explainer: Treble damages (WA IFCA) or 2–3x multiplier with minimal procedural friction.

levels.name: No fee shifting
levels.explainer: American rule applies; insureds pay their own fees regardless of outcome.

levels.name: Narrow / discretionary fee shift
levels.explainer: Fees available only in narrow circumstances or at court discretion; not routinely awarded.

levels.name: Statutory fee shift, conditions apply
levels.explainer: One-way fee shift to prevailing insured under specified statutory conditions.

levels.name: Broad one-way fee shift
levels.explainer: Fees routinely available to prevailing insured under bad-faith statute or common-law doctrine.

levels.name: Mandatory fees + costs + multipliers
levels.explainer: Mandatory fee award plus expert costs; some jurisdictions allow lodestar multipliers.

levels.name: Heavy mandatory exhaustion + cure
levels.explainer: Mandatory long cure period plus dual exhaustion plus strict notice (e.g., MD admin-channel hybrid).

levels.name: Mandatory cure period
levels.explainer: Mandatory 30–60 day cure period with formal notice/demand requirement.

levels.name: Light notice, no cure
levels.explainer: Some pre-suit notice required but no mandatory cure window.

levels.name: No pre-suit barrier
levels.explainer: Insured may file immediately upon denial; no notice or cure required.

levels.name: Minimal admin role
levels.explainer: DOI does not investigate individual claim disputes or take substantive action on them.

levels.name: Logging only, no findings
levels.explainer: DOI tracks complaints but does not issue substantive findings or take enforcement action on individual claims.

levels.name: Substantive findings + fining authority
levels.explainer: DOI investigates claims, issues findings, and has meaningful fining authority over insurer conduct.

levels.name: Quasi-adjudicatory channel
levels.explainer: DOI provides a structured pre-suit or parallel quasi-adjudication channel with weighted findings (e.g., MD §27-1001).

levels.name: Sustained narrowing
levels.explainer: Recent legislation or appellate decisions have narrowed insured-side remedies (FL SB 2A, LA Act 3, TX HB 1774).

levels.name: Stable / mixed
levels.explainer: No clear directional trend over the last ~10 years; doctrinal status quo.

levels.name: Sustained broadening
levels.explainer: Recent legislation or appellate decisions have expanded insured-side remedies.
